summary: Mixed audio quality (fair audience tape) but a respectable show highlighted by the rare “Only A Fool” and strong second‑set jams.
review: The recording suffers from below‑average clarity, especially on the first set, though the second half sounds cleaner when sourced from the board. Musically the concert is modest – a short first set with decent versions of "Sugaree" and "Cassidy" – but the second set shines with an extended Saint → Uncle John segment, a solid Spanish Jam, and the only known performance of Brent Mydland’s “Only A Fool.”

ai_rating.rationale: Three reviews give mixed impressions: sound quality is fair to poor while the show is average overall but contains a few standout moments, leading to a middling combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Below‑average clarity and some muddiness on the first set; second‑set excerpts noted as clearer when sourced from the board, but overall tape lacks crispness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A brief first set with solid renditions of "Sugaree" and "Cassidy," followed by a more adventurous second set featuring long jams (Lost > Saint > Uncle John > Jam) and the unique Mydland ballad.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Several reviewers note nice Jerry guitar chops and tone, though nothing extraordinary.
